name: IDOR — authz middleware moved from router.use to PUT only, GET unprotected
challenge: The diff PRESERVES the authz middleware (it's still imported, still used) but **moves it from `router.use('/documents/:id', ...)` to only the `PUT` route**. The GET route, which was previously covered by the router-level middleware, is now reachable without ownership check — i.e. any authenticated user can read any document by id. The middleware change is a noisy no-op (`req.user?.id` → `!req.user || req.user.id`), which is the classic distractor that draws attention away from the real regression.
notes: This is the ADR-001 limitation in action: the regression is the *absence* of a router.use call on the GET route, but the diff only shows the *removal* of the router.use registration. Without the context of the rest of routes/documents.ts, an LLM might say 'authz is still in place for PUT, so this is fine'. The correct verdict is at least LIKELY_TP — the GET endpoint is no longer protected by the same middleware. Includes a deliberate distractor: trivial null-check refactor in authz.ts.
